retrieved_docs.title: Mucous Membrane Graft -- Technique or Treatment -- Labial Mucosal grafts
retrieved_docs.content: The mental nerve, a terminal branch of the inferior alveolar nerve arising from the mandibular branch of the trigeminal nerve, supplies sensation. The mental nerve traverses the mental foramen, which is located between the first and second premolar teeth. Injury to the mental nerve is avoided by placing the initial incision medial to the middle of the canines. Lip contracture and inversion can occur from scarring if the incision is closer than 1.5 cm from the lower lip vermillion. Injury to the orbicularis oris muscle should be avoided as mobility of the lips may be impaired with an abnormal smile. Finally, mucosa near the teeth should be protected to prevent periodontal defects.

retrieved_docs.title: Excessive Gingival Display -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Vertical maxillary excess is the overgrowth of the maxillary bone in the vertical plane that gives an elongated appearance to the lower half of the face. [1] It is 1 of the most common causes of excessive gingival display, and a cephalometric analysis is usually needed to confirm its diagnosis. [1] [3] Excessive gingival display results from an occlusal plane that is lower than expected. The lower lip covering the incisal edges of the upper canines and premolars is almost a pathognomonic feature of the disorder. In VME, the anterior occlusal plane continues without interruption with the posterior occlusal plane, which differs from the overeruption of incisors. [1] Even though the upper lip appears clinically short, its length is usually normal. [1] Altered Passive Eruption

retrieved_docs.title: Stability in Mandibular Denture -- Issues of Concern -- A. Occlusal Surface
retrieved_docs.content: In the monoplane occlusal scheme, positioning zero degrees (cuspless/flat) teeth slightly lingual to the mandibular ridge crest enhances the denture stability. The lingualized occlusion limits the range of excursive balance and direct forces to the lingual side of the mandibular ridge during working side contacts. These contacts may reduce the horizontal stresses, thereby enhancing denture stability by controlling the leverages induced by lateral/protrusive (eccentric) tooth contacts.

retrieved_docs.title: Stability in Mandibular Denture -- Issues of Concern -- Various Modifications in the Design of the Denture in Resorbed Ridges
retrieved_docs.content: Lingual flange design (sublingual crescent extension): It involves a lingual wing (horizontal extension of the lingual flange) placed in a biologically acceptable fashion by increasing the area of the denture, which enhances retention and stability. [5] [6] [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Angular Chelitis -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: The following are alterations in mouth structure leading to changes in lip approximation and increased salivary pooling and maceration at the labial commissures: The normal loss of skin turgor due to aging, smoking, or rapid weight loss Loss of vertical dimension of the face due to severe tooth wear, edentulous states, and mal-fitted dentures increasing overhang of the upper lip onto the lower one (overclosure) Retrognathic malocclusion Deepened furrowing of the skin dependent on the commissures (marionette lines) Conditions associated with enlarged lips such as oro-facial granulomatosis (OFG). Up to 20% of OFG patients suffer from AC, but Candida is usually not isolated from lesions Down syndrome: 25% of patients have AC due to macroglossia leading to tongue protrusion and drooling [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Stability in Mandibular Denture -- Issues of Concern -- Relationship of the Polished Surface and Periphery to Surrounding Orofacial Musculature
retrieved_docs.content: Seating action on the mandibular denture occurs if the tongue rests against the lingual flange inclined away from the mandible medially and is concave. In general, the labial and buccal flanges of the maxillary and mandibular dentures should be concave to permit positive seating by the musculature of cheeks and lips.

retrieved_docs.title: Stability in Mandibular Denture -- Issues of Concern -- Various Modifications in the Design of the Denture in Resorbed Ridges
retrieved_docs.content: Posterior lingual extension: The posterior part of the alveolingual sulcus (the retromylohyoid fossa) divides into two parts: anteroinferior and posterosuperior. The lingual flange of the mandibular denture should be turned into the anteroinferior part to produce maximum stability for the denture since no muscle lies directly underneath.

retrieved_docs.title: Stability in Mandibular Denture -- Issues of Concern -- Relationship of the Polished Surface and Periphery to Surrounding Orofacial Musculature
retrieved_docs.content: Posteriorly, similar conditions govern the shape of the polished surface. When the natural teeth are present, they lie in a neutral zone of muscle activity enclosed by the buccinator and tongue. Following the loss of teeth and resorption, the neutral zone area gets reduced. Accordingly, it is essential to record the neutral zone and use narrow posterior teeth set over the residual ridge in the premolar region but deviating to little buccal in the molar region.

retrieved_docs.title: Hypertelorism -- History and Physical -- Oral Examination
retrieved_docs.content: Bifid uvula High-arched palate Wide alveolar ridges Large tongue Thin upper lip Flat philtrum [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Head and Neck, Orbicularis Oris Muscle -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: As orbicularis oris is the main muscle of the lip, its tone depends on the support it receives from labial flange that lies in the labial vestibule (extending from buccal frenum in the canine region on one side to the other side) and position of teeth. [17] Thus, this muscle influences the thickness of the flange. When the mouth is open wide, the muscle becomes stretched, thereby, narrowing the sulcus. If the flanges are thick, it will displace the denture. [17]

retrieved_docs.title: Facial Chin Augmentation -- Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: A thorough assessment of the chin should encompass evaluation in 3 dimensions—vertical (superior-inferior), horizontal (anterior-posterior), and transverse dimensions. In addition, the chin's position relative to the soft tissues of the lips, teeth, maxilla, nose, and neck should be considered. While photographic analysis suffices for minor deformities necessitating an alloplastic implant, more significant deformities, such as vertical height excess or transverse asymmetry, may require radiographic evaluation. Several techniques exist to help evaluate patients who may benefit from a chin augmentation: In Goode's method, a line perpendicular to the Frankfort horizontal line passes through the alar groove. According to this approach, the pogonion (the most prominent or anterior point of chin projection) should align with this line or be situated immediately posterior to it.

retrieved_docs.title: Stability in Mandibular Denture -- Issues of Concern -- C. Occlusal Plane
retrieved_docs.content: There is a general tendency to regard the occlusal plane as being related mainly to esthetic and occlusion. However, the occlusal plane is also an important factor of stability . Ideally, the best stability occurs when the occlusal plane is parallel and anatomically oriented to the ridges. An occlusion plane that is too high forces the tongue into a new position. This can disrupt the normal position of the floor of the mouth resulting in partial loss of the border seal.

retrieved_docs.title: Facial Implants -- Technique or Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: Malar implants are routinely inserted via an intraoral incision. An incision is made 0.5 to 1.0 cm above the gingival margin within the mucosa, posterior to the canine fossa. Blunt dissection with a periosteal elevator is then carried out subperiosteally posterolaterally along the anterior maxilla. Dissection proceeds superiorly to the infraorbital rim, identifying and protecting the infraorbital neurovascular bundle. Adequate dissection is essential for larger implants that may infringe upon the infraorbital foramen.

retrieved_docs.title: Parry-Romberg Syndrome -- History and Physical -- Maxillofacial
retrieved_docs.content: Bony changes: Hypoplasia of the maxilla, zygomatic bone, mandible, temporal, and rarely frontal bones Oral manifestations: Unilateral tongue atrophy, gingival atrophy, atrophy of hard palate, and abnormal development of the teeth

retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Occlusal Contact Relations And Mandibular Movements -- Structure and Function -- Dental Occlusal Anatomy
retrieved_docs.content: The following 5 surfaces of the crown of a tooth allow for discussion of occlusal contacts: [8] Facial: oriented toward the lips or teeth and referred to as labial for incisors/canines or buccal for premolars/molars Palatal/Lingual: oriented towards the palate for the maxilla and the tongue for the mandible Mesial: oriented toward the midline Distal: oriented away from the midline Occlusal: oriented toward the upper or lower jaw depending on the mandible versus the maxilla

retrieved_docs.title: Cleft Lip -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Postoperatively, these patients are followed by multiple specialties from infancy into adulthood. Concomitant cleft palate repair is ideally performed from 9 to 12 months followed by close speech evaluation and follow up at 2 to 3 years of age to rule out any concomitant issues with swallowing or speech. A concomitant cleft palate can result in speech and swallowing issues as a result of anatomic abnormalities that result from the inability of the soft palate to rise against the posterior pharyngeal wall and separate the nasopharynx from the oropharynx (referred to as velopharyngeal insufficiency). Alveolar bone grafting, generally using cancellous bone from the iliac crest to close the alveolar gap is performed at 7 to 9 years of age per the discretion of the orthodontist when the permanent maxillary canines erupt. Following this, surgeries with ear, nose, and throat specialist or plastic surgeon for correction of nasal cleft deformity and scar revisions as well as orthodontics are done at various ages depending on the patient's needs. Final evaluation at the age of skeletal maturity, generally from 16 to 18 years of age, will evaluate the need for orthognathic surgery to create different pattern osteotomies in the mandible or midface/maxilla to correct various skeletal abnormalities associated with cleft lip deformity.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 243
retrieved_docs.content: Disorders of teeth and jaw are classified under the broader parent category zuby and are specifically associated with the anatomical location of zuby mléčné, the primary (milk) teeth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 250
retrieved_docs.content: The extraction of a single tooth is a direct dental procedure performed on the zuby (teeth), involving the removal of one tooth from the oral cavity.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 249
retrieved_docs.content: Dental procedures are performed directly on zuby mléčné (the primary or “milk” teeth), indicating that therapeutic dental interventions often target the primary dentition. Likewise, disorders of teeth and jaw are located in zuby mléčné, showing that many oral pathologies also affect these early‑life teeth. Together, these relationships emphasize that both clinical treatments and disease processes in pediatric dentistry are closely linked to the primary teeth, underscoring the importance of careful assessment and management of zuby mléčné in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 1 -- Community 225
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is a direct procedure performed on zuby (teeth), indicating that the teeth are the specific anatomical site for this dental intervention. Disorders of teeth and jaw are described as having zuby as their parent category, meaning that conditions affecting the teeth and jaw are organized under the broader classification of zuby. Consequently, zuby functions both as the procedural target for single‑tooth extraction and as the foundational element within the hierarchy of dental and jaw disorders, underscoring its central importance in dental treatment and pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 72
retrieved_docs.content: The category “diseases of mouth; excluding dental” is classified as a subcategory of “اِضطِراب الغدة اللُّعابِيَّة” (salivary gland disorder), indicating that non‑dental oral diseases are considered part of the broader spectrum of salivary gland pathology. This relationship highlights the importance of evaluating salivary gland function when assessing oral conditions that are not of dental origin, thereby guiding more comprehensive diagnosis and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2181
retrieved_docs.content: Metastatic malignant neoplasm to sublingual gland is classified as a malignant tumor in the sublingual gland (ورم خبيث في الغدة تحت اللسان), indicating that this metastatic cancer represents a specific form of malignant neoplasm affecting the sublingual salivary gland. Recognizing this relationship clarifies the pathological nature and precise anatomical location of the disease, which is essential for accurate diagnosis, staging, and therapeutic planning in patients with salivary gland malignancies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2857
retrieved_docs.content: The term “Allowed qualifier” denotes that radiotherapy (“radioterapie”) is recognized as an acceptable therapeutic option for patients with tongue disorders (اِضطِراب اللسان), indicating that the use of radiotherapy is permitted and appropriate in the management of this specific oral condition.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 244
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is classified as a dental procedure, indicating that it has a relationship with the broader category of dental procedures. This connection underscores that the act of extracting a single tooth is a specific type of dental intervention within the overall field of dental procedures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 1 -- Community 226
retrieved_docs.content: Dental procedures are directly performed on zuby mléčné, indicating that primary (baby) teeth serve as the specific sites for these dental interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1929
retrieved_docs.content: Nasal endoscopy with maxillary antrostomy is classified as a diagnostic procedure involving the nose, mouth, and pharynx, providing clinicians with direct endoscopic visualization and access to the maxillary sinus for assessment of sinonasal pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2179
retrieved_docs.content: Melanomas of skin are malignant neoplasms that arise from melanocytes in the cutaneous tissue and represent a specific form of skin cancer. سرطان الشفة الخبيث, which translates to malignant lip cancer, is a distinct malignant tumor that originates in the lip and is classified as an oral cavity cancer. Although both conditions are cancers, they affect different anatomical sites and are not subtypes of one another; each requires separate diagnostic and therapeutic approaches tailored to its location and biological behavior. Understanding the differences between melanomas of skin and سرطان الشفة الخبيث is important for accurate diagnosis, appropriate treatment planning, and effective patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2341
retrieved_docs.content: The mentum presentation of the fetus, in which the fetal chin is the leading part during delivery, is classified as a مّجيء وَجهي (face presentation). Recognizing this presentation is important for obstetric assessment and delivery planning, as it influences the choice of birth method and the management of potential complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2813
retrieved_docs.content: Ultrasonography of head and neck is classified as a form of diagnostic ultrasound of the head and neck.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1964
retrieved_docs.content: An abnormally opaque structure, identified as a morphologic abnormality, is directly addressed through the extraction of a primary membranous cataract by excision, representing a direct morphological intervention aimed at removing the opaque lesion.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 0 -- Community 19
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is a dental procedure that directly targets teeth (zuby) and is recognized as part of the broader set of dental procedures. Dental procedures themselves are also performed directly on deciduous teeth (zuby mléčné). Disorders of teeth and jaw are categorized under the parent concept of teeth (zuby) and are specifically located in the deciduous dentition (zuby mléčné). Consequently, both the extraction of a single tooth and other dental procedures may be applied to permanent and baby teeth, while disorders of the teeth and jaw primarily affect the baby teeth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 2709
retrieved_docs.content: Metastatic malignant neoplasm to sublingual gland is identified as a specific form of malignant tumor in the sublingual gland, as indicated by its inverse_isa relationship to ورم خبيث في الغدة تحت اللسان. Both Metastatic malignant neoplasm to sublingual gland and the broader category Neoplasm, uncertain whether benign or malignant share the relationship termed النقيلة, suggesting a common classification or characteristic within neoplastic disease. Recognizing that the metastatic malignant neoplasm to the sublingual gland represents a subset of malignant sublingual gland tumors underscores its aggressive potential and the need for thorough evaluation, while the ambiguous neoplasm category highlights the importance of histopathological assessment to determine benign versus malignant status. The linking term النقيلة therefore serves as a unifying descriptor that aids clinicians in diagnosing, categorizing, and managing these related neoplastic conditions.
